english: downward, to what is earthly. -- *mig-śóg* (mig shog) W. eye-lash. -- mig-śér (mig sher) 1. jaundice, also *gya-nág mig-śér* (gya nag mig sher) W. 2. envy, jealousy, mig-śér-čan (mig sher can) envious, jealous. -- mig-hu-ré (mig hu re) v. hu-re (hu re).

resp. mtsan (mtshan), name, kyód-kyi miṅ či yin (khyod kyi ming ci yin) Mil. or *či zer* (ci zer) W. what is your name? dei miṅ yaṅ med (de'i ming yang med) Glr. such a thing is or was not known at all, such a thing does not exist; miṅ-tsam-gyi dge-slóṅ (ming tsam gyi dge slong) Dzl. priest only by name; W.: *miṅ-gi náṅ-na* (ming gi nang na) id.; C. also: *ṭál-gyi miṅ tsám-le me* (tral gyi ming tsam le me) this tax exists only nominally; appellation, designation, word, téṅ-pai miṅ (theng pa'i ming) a word for drawing (pulling) Gram.; miṅ-gi mdzod (ming gi mdzod) dictionary; *kyod-sụ miṅ daṅ* (khyod su ming dang) or *sụ miṅ-ni náṅ-na* (su ming ni nang na) or *sụ miṅ nén-te* (su ming nen te) or *sụ miṅ-la téṅ-te ča dug* (su ming la theng te ca dug) W. in whose name or business, upon whose order are you going? *čii miṅ daṅ* (ci'i ming dang) W. for what cause, in behalf of what affair? miṅ-nas rjód-pa (ming nas rjod pa), or smó-ba (smo ba) Dzl. and elsewh., to call by name, also to call upon the name of, hence . . . kyi miṅ-nas brjód-de (... kyi ming nas brjod de) in the name of; miṅ dógspa (ming 'dogs pa) to name v. miṅ (ming) 2; dṅós-miṅ (dngos ming) v. dṅos (dngos); btágs-miṅ (btags ming) a name given (e.g. a Christian name) Cs., rjes-grúb-kyi miṅ (rjes grub kyi ming) a surname Cs., rus-miṅ (rus ming) a family name Cs. Comp. miṅ-rkyáṅ (ming rkyang) a single syllable or name Cs., cf. miṅ-sbyár (ming sbyar). -- miṅ-grógs (ming grogs) one's name-sake Cs. -- miṅ-sgrá (ming sgra) a mere name, word, or sound (philosophical term.) Was. -- miṅ-ṅán (ming ngan) a bad name, infamy Cs. -- miṅ-čan (ming can) having a name, dpal-˳byór miṅ-čan (dpal 'byor ming can) one of the name of Paljor. -- miṅ-ton (ming 'ton) v. ˳tón-pa ('ton pa). -- miṅ-mtá (ming mtha') final letter Cs. -- miṅ-sbyár (ming sbyar) compound name. -- miṅ-méd (ming med) 1. nameless. 2. the fourth finger. -- miṅ-tsig (ming tshig) word, appellation. -- miṅ-yżi (ming gzhi) the first letter of the root of a word, in contradistinction to the second, the third, and the prefix-letters, miṅ-yżi rkyáṅ-pa (ming gzhi rkyang pa) a single initial, e.g. ཀ, including ཀྐ, ཀྵ, Zam.; ynyis-sbrél (gnyis sbrel), ysum-sbrél (gsum sbrel) a double, triple, letter, like རྐ, སྐྱ, Cs. (?) -- miṅ-bzáṅ (ming bzang) good reputation Cs.

brother in relation to his sister, miṅ-sriṅ (ming sring) brother and sister; de ṅa daṅ miṅ-sriṅ-du byao (de nga dang ming sring du bya'o) Dzl. her and myself I shall make to be sister and brother, i.e. I shall raise her to be my sister.
a large fish Cs.; mid-míd (mid mid) id.
1. sbst. gullet, oesophagus Mil. and elsewh.; mid-skrán (mid skran) a tumour of it, incident to horses Sch. -- 2. vb. to swallow, to gulp down, frq.

W. *man* (man), 1. for ma yin (ma yin) (he, she, it) is not, śa-min-tsil-min (sha min tshil min) Mil. they are neither 'flesh nor fat'. -- 2. abbrev. for min-pa (min pa) and min-par (min par) v. below; btaṅ-min (btang min) for *btaṅ yin-nam ma yin* (btang yin nam ma yin) W. will it be given or not? min-pa (min pa) and ma yin-pa (ma yin pa) to be not; often as a participle supplying the place of a prep. or adv. (for min-par (min par)), excepted, except, besides, de ma yin-pai śiṅ (de ma yin pa'i shing) Stg. the other trees except this one; klu ma yin-pa yżan mi tub (klu ma yin pa gzhan mi thub) Dzl. except he that is a Lu cannot . . .; saṅs-rgyás min-pa sus kyaṅ mi śes (sangs rgyas min pa sus kyang mi shes) Mil. besides Buddha no one knows of it, no one knows it except Buddha; ṅas yug yčig min-pa mi bsdad (ngas yug gcig min pa mi bsdad) Mil. I have been sitting down only this moment; ro zér-ba min-pa skyab-pai miṅ mi yoṅ-ba ˳dug (ro zer ba min pa skyab pa'i ming mi yong ba 'dug) Mil. one can only say 'corpse', and the appellation 'skyab-pa' (skyab pa) is not admissible; de min (de min) besides, otherwise, else, apart from, setting aside Mil.; even: de-min-rnams (de min rnams) Glr. those that are not doing so. Cf. man (man).
v. mi-mda (mi mda'), sub mi (mi) compounds.
the Hind. mēṃ, Madam, mim sá-heb (mim sa heb) the mistress or lady of the house.
termin., མིས་ mis (mis) instrum. case of mi (mi).

1. num. fig.: 76. -- 2. sbst. border, boundary, limit, edge, end, żiṅ-mu-la gnás-pai lha (zhing mu la gnas pa'i lha) deity residing on the land-mark; mú-la skye (mu la skye) (the plant) grows on the edges of fields Wdṅ.; mta méd-čiṅ mu med (mtha' med cing mu med) Stg. there is neither limit nor end; mu bżi = mta bżi (mu bzhi = mtha' bzhi) Mil., S.g. seems to be used in a philosophical sense for 'perfect limitedness'; mu-kyúd (mu khyud) circumference, compass, the hoops of a cask Sch., the rim of a wheel Stg.; mu-kyúd-˳dzin (mu khyud 'dzin) n.p., the least of the
